title: Establish bounded screen-reader product support
goal: Audit, repair and physically test the public globe with a named screen reader so selection, panels, layers and the complete linear fallback have usable semantics without making unsupported cross-platform claims.

approval.note: Use an isolated Commonworld worktree. Before merge require a complete downloadable diff, head- and diff-bound self-review, green CI and exact post-merge live validation.

acceptance.assertion: The map shell, digital-sphere controls, layer controls, focus panel, status messages and complete linear catalog have an explicit semantic and focus model.
A project selected from every supported surface resolves to the same CommonProject identity and focus-panel content, and closing the panel returns focus predictably.
At least one named physical screen-reader path, device, OS and browser is exercised against an exact commit; all steps, failures and unsupported gestures are recorded.
Automated accessibility checks and keyboard tests cover regressions but are not treated as substitutes for the physical assistive-technology run.
The result is bounded to the tested platform and does not claim cross-platform screen-reader support, full WCAG conformance or accessibility correctness beyond the checked flows.

operator_disposition.reason: The operator chose to leave bounded screen-reader product support for a later decision.
